data.title: JPMorgan expands in Dubai as Middle East competition heats up
data.description:
Key points:
  • JPMorgan targets mid-cap growth in Europe, Middle East
  • Citigroup sees UAE as key market and is investing
  • JPMorgan weighs expanding footprint in Turkey

By Tom Sims

The U.S. bank JPMorgan has expanded in Dubai as part of a broader push to grow and do more business with medium-sized companies, an executive told Reuters, as competition in the Middle East intensifies.

The move, not previously reported, is a challenge to competitors such as Citigroup , and comes after JPMorgan recently devoted more resources to coverage of so-called midcaps in Austria and Poland.

"There's a global focus on doing more in the midcap space," Stefan Povaly, London-based co-head of corporate banking for Europe, the Middle East and Africa, said. Midcaps give JPMorgan another revenue stream beyond its traditional focus on the biggest blue-chip firms.

"The Middle East is of course a priority ... This is the first step for an expansion into the midcap space," Povaly said.

Global financial firms have increasingly set up operations in the Middle East to tap into oil wealth and growing regional markets. Barclays recently announced an expansion into Saudi Arabia, while Goldman Sachs opened an office in Kuwait.

Citigroup first opened in the UAE in 1964, and added commercial bank activities in 2007.

Speaking about competition in general, Alex Stiris, head of Citi's commercial banking in Europe, the Middle East and Africa, told Reuters that Citi sees the UAE as a location with one of the greatest opportunities for increasing market share and that his bank has an ingrained "natural advantage".

"We have seen more competition come into the UAE," he said, not referring to any individual firm.

"Obviously the more competition, the more we have to be on our tiptoes. So it worries me to some extent," he said. "We can't rest on our laurels."

“We are investing selectively," Stiris said. "Investing is not just in terms of just adding more headcount. It's also in terms of just looking at the people we have, and in some cases, just upgrading people."

"There's also investing in terms of capital,” he added.

Elsewhere, JPMorgan is in the early stages of evaluating a move to increase covering midcaps in Turkey.

"Over time, we could look to hire bankers dedicated to midcap clients in the country," Povaly said.

JPMorgan has relocated Tushar Arora, a banker who has been with the U.S. lender for more than a decade, from London to take up a Dubai role as the first in a team to focus on smaller venture capital-backed companies.

The activity follows a push to Poland with the hire of Marcin Pietrucha from Santander, who has built a team based in Warsaw and other hubs.

In parallel, JPMorgan is seeking more midcap business in Austria, headed by banker Philippe Bull based in Frankfurt.

JPMorgan has a large presence in Germany, in part for coverage of the country's medium-sized Mittelstand firms.

JPMorgan has been on an expansion course in Europe. The bank this week officially opened an office in Berlin with space for 400 staff ahead of its launch of a digital retail bank.

Last week, German regulators imposed a record fine on JPMorgan for deficiencies in its anti-money laundering controls, reflecting the scale of its operations in the country.

data.title: REG - Crystal Amber Fund - Transaction in Own Shares and Total Voting Rights
data.description:
RNS Number : 6848H Crystal Amber Fund Limited 14 November 2025  

14 November 2025

Crystal Amber Fund Limited

("Crystal Amber Fund", the "Company" or the "Fund")

Transaction in Own Shares and

Change to Total Voting Rights

The Board of Crystal Amber Fund Limited announces that, in accordance with the authority granted by shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting on 13 December 2024, on 14 November 2025 the Company purchased 1,636,847 of its own ordinary shares of 1p each ("Ordinary Shares") at a price of £1.45 per share. These shares will be cancelled.

In accordance with Article 5(1)(b) of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 (the Market Abuse Regulation), the schedule at the end of this announcement contains detailed information about the individual purchases made by Winterflood Securities on behalf of the Company as part of the buyback programme.

Total Voting Rights  

Following this transaction, the total number of Ordinary Shares in issue at today's date is 84,623,762 and following this transaction, the total number of Ordinary Shares held as treasury shares by the Company is 23,468,262, (which includes 6,949,500 shares purchased by the Company which are pending cancellation). Therefore, the total number of voting rights in the Company is 61,155,500 which may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest or a change to their interest in the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: Nordicus Partners Corp SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Nordicus Partners Corp, a business accelerator and holding company for Nordic life sciences companies aiming to enter the American market, has released its Form 10-Q report for the quarter ended September 30, 2025. The report provides a detailed overview of the company's financial performance and significant business developments during the period.

Financial Highlights

Revenue: The company did not generate any revenue during the three and six months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $2,500 in the same periods in 2024.

Loss from Operations: $(1.21) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.51) million for the same period in 2024, primarily due to increased operating expenses.

Net Loss: $(1.51) million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.51) million for the same period in 2024, reflecting higher operating expenses and changes in fair value of investments.

Net Loss Per Share: $(0.08) for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.10) for the same period in 2024, due to an increase in the weighted average number of shares outstanding.

Net Loss: $(2.72) million for the six months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.77) million for the same period in 2024, driven by increased research and development expenses and professional fees.

Net Loss Per Share: $(0.15) for the six months ended September 30, 2025, compared to $(0.19) for the same period in 2024, reflecting a higher number of shares outstanding.

Business Highlights

Organizational History: Nordicus Partners Corporation, originally founded in 1993, has undergone several transformations, including a name change to Nordicus Partners Corporation in May 2023. The company has shifted its focus to becoming a business accelerator and holding company for Nordic life sciences companies aiming to enter the American market.

Business Model and Strategy: Nordicus Partners Corporation employs a 4-step value creation process: Scout and Accelerate, Acquire and Exit. The company targets high-impact potential companies, providing capital and resources to drive critical milestones, and acquires controlling stakes to maximize value creation.

Portfolio Companies: The current portfolio includes Orocidin A/S, Bio-Convert A/S, and NoviThera ApS. These companies are focused on developing innovative treatments for aggressive periodontitis, oral leukoplakia, and psoriasis, respectively.

Orocidin A/S Developments: Orocidin A/S has completed a 14-day toxicology study in hamsters and a Beagle Dog Study, showing high tolerance and effectiveness of its drug QR-01 for periodontitis, marking significant progress towards human clinical trials.

Bio-Convert A/S Developments: Bio-Convert A/S received positive feedback from the Danish Medicines Agency for its QR-02 treatment for oral leukoplakia, paving the way for a First in Human trial.

NoviThera ApS Formation: NoviThera ApS was formed in October 2025 to develop a novel monoclonal antibody treatment for psoriasis, leveraging intellectual property from Alteral Therapeutics.

Future Outlook: Nordicus aims to take its portfolio companies through Phase I clinical trials, with potential options for sale, merger, further development, strategic partnerships, or IPOs. The company is focused on backing high-growth ventures and transformative innovations in the life sciences sector.

SEC Filing: Nordicus Partners Corp [ NORD ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: AMERICAN SHARED HOSPITAL SERVICES SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
American Shared Hospital Services, a provider of turnkey technology solutions for advanced radiosurgical and radiation therapy services, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in both financial performance and operational metrics, reflecting the company's strategic initiatives and operational efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

Total Revenue: The company reported total revenue of $7.171 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, an increase of $172,000 compared to the same period in the prior year. This growth was primarily driven by higher direct patient services revenue.

Gross Margin: Gross margin for the quarter was $1.586 million, up by $216,000 from the same period last year. The improvement reflects enhanced efficiency in direct patient services.

Operating Loss: Operating loss for the quarter was $(344,000), a significant improvement from $(889,000) in the same period last year. This reduction in loss was due to decreased selling and administrative expenses.

Net (Loss) Income: The company reported a net loss of $(329,000) for the quarter, compared to a net loss of $(410,000) in the same period last year. The improvement is attributed to a decrease in interest expense and an improved gross margin.

Net (Loss) Income Attributable to American Shared Hospital Services: The net loss attributable to the company was $(17,000) for the quarter, a significant reduction from $(207,000) in the same period last year.

(Loss) Income Per Common Share - Basic: The basic loss per common share was $(0.00) for the quarter, compared to $(0.03) in the same period last year, reflecting improved financial performance.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: American Shared Hospital Services operates two main segments: leasing and direct patient services. The leasing segment includes Gamma Knife and PBRT systems, while the direct patient services segment includes facilities in Rhode Island, Peru, Ecuador, and Mexico.

Geographical Performance: The company expanded its direct patient services segment with the acquisition of three radiation therapy facilities in Rhode Island and the opening of a new facility in Puebla, Mexico. The Rhode Island facilities contributed significantly to the increase in direct patient services revenue.

Sales Units: Gamma Knife procedures increased by 13 to 231 for the three-month period ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same period last year. However, the number of procedures decreased by 128 to 703 for the nine-month period.

New Production Launches: The company completed an equipment upgrade in Peru to a Gamma Knife Esprit in June 2025, which led to increased volume due to shorter treatment times.

Future Outlook: Looking ahead, the company plans to install two Leksell Gamma Knife Esprit Systems and two Linear Accelerator systems in 2026 or later, indicating a focus on expanding its equipment offerings and capacity.

SEC Filing: AMERICAN SHARED HOSPITAL SERVICES [ AMS ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Here's what the U.S.-China trade truce really means for the U.S. soybean market - and for farmers
data.description:
By Myra P. Saefong

U.S. farmers may never regain the market share lost to rivals in Brazil and elsewhere

Instead of buying soybeans from the U.S., China has turned to other sources such as Brazil to meet its needs.

China has made a pledge to buy U.S. soybeans over the next few years, fueling hope for farmers who have been caught in the middle of a trade war between the two nations.

Yet the damage to the U.S. agricultural market may have already been done.

While China has turned its attention to alternative sources such as Brazil, the U.S. and its farmers have fewer options, having relied on China to buy around half of American soybean exports for years.

"We lost market share in the last trade war that we never gained back," said Faith Parum, an economist at American Farm Bureau Federation. The last major trade war between the U.S. and China began in 2018, during President Donald Trump's first term in the White House, and has continued during his second term.

"China's total soybean demand stayed strong, but they've been filling most of that from Brazil over the last few years," Parum told MarketWatch. Brazil alone has shipped around 2.5 billion bushels to China this year, while U.S. exports have been relatively small, she said.

Under a deal with the White House announced on Nov. 1, China will buy at least 12 million metric tons of U.S. soybeans over the last two months of this year. It will also purchase at least 25 metric tons of U.S. soybeans in each of the next three years. Prior to the agreement, China had not purchased soybeans from the U.S. for the current marketing year, which began in September.

China's purchase commitment "takes us back to where things were before the trade war," said Parum. "It's a strong starting point, and actual purchases could exceed those minimums if market conditions remain favorable."

If the agreement is fully implemented, it could help strengthen soybean prices for U.S. farmers, she said. It could, however, also "influence how China sources soybeans from South America."

Market share

Brazil and Argentina have become major suppliers of soybeans, and it will be important to see "whether China diversifies purchases or continues to rely primarily on Brazil," Parum said.

Arlan Suderman, chief commodities economist at StoneX, said China has been investing in Brazilian agriculture and infrastructure for the past two decades, "supporting development that would make Brazil the low-cost provider of corn and soybeans that it is today."

As Brazil increases production, China buys more from it, Suderman noted, adding that things are nearing a point where "Brazil, with little help from Argentina and others, could meet all of China's import needs - and largely do it at cheaper prices."

Trump's tariffs may have sped up that cycle somewhat, but things were quickly headed in that direction anyway, Suderman said. New-crop soybeans from Brazil will almost certainly be cheaper than U.S. soybeans once harvest begins there in a couple of months, he added, citing currency exchange-rate differences.

U.S. soybeans were actually priced cheaper than Brazilian soybeans delivered to ports in China before the U.S.-China agreement - but U.S. soybean futures prices rallied following the deal's announcement, and Brazilian old-crop supplies are now cheaper than U.S. soybeans loaded out at the Gulf of Mexico (which the U.S. now calls the Gulf of America) and shipped to China, said Suderman.

China left a 10% retaliatory tariff in place on U.S. soybeans, which means U.S. soybeans delivered to China are still roughly $1.50 more per bushel than Brazilian soybeans, he said. At the time he made his comments, U.S. soybean prices were trading at around $11.20 a bushel. Soybeans for January delivery (SF26) (S00) trading in Chicago settled at $11.47 on Thursday.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture has forecast soybean exports of 1.64 billion bushels for the 2025-26 marketing year, down 50 million bushels from the October forecast, according to the monthly report from the government agency released Friday.

"While U.S. soybean exports are expected to rise to China for the rest of the marketing year, these higher shipments could be offset by reductions to other markets where the United States no longer holds a large price discount compared to other exporters," the USDA said in its report.

Caught in the middle

Some soybean farmers weren't very impressed by the latest U.S. deal with China.

"I don't think soybean purchases by China will ever return to pretariff levels," Ed Hodgson, who farms 1,500 acres of land in Rice County, Kan., told MarketWatch. "The low-cost producing country will fill China's needs."

'I don't think soybean purchases by China will ever return to pretariff levels.'Ed Hodgson, Kansas farmer

Even if a mooted $13 billion bailout happens, "it just helps us get to the end of the year," Hodgson said. "There won't be anything left in most farmers' pockets to get us into the 2026 planting season."

There's been speculation that the Trump administration will offer a bailout to farmers. Trump was considering providing $10 billion or more in aid to U.S. farmers, the Wall Street Journal reported early last month, citing people familiar with the discussions.

In late October, Sen. John Hoeven of North Dakota told reporters that a market facilitation program, similar to the farmer bailout Trump issued during his first-term trade wars, was ready but was being delayed by the government shutdown, according to a report from Politico.

Also read: Trump floats using tariff money to help farmers hurt by trade wars

"Unfortunately, nothing has truly been 'gained' for our farmers," said Jake Hanley, managing director and senior portfolio specialist at Teucrium. "They remain caught in the middle of a trade war where they are a vulnerable target."

Soybean prices rallied on optimism following the U.S. trade announcement earlier this month, but Chinese buyers continue to favor Brazilian beans, Hanley noted.

"So this is a 'show me the money' moment for grain markets," he said, adding that the rally in soybeans "needs confirmation, not just hope."

What's next?

The good news is that global demand for soybeans remains strong and other markets are growing, said Parum of the American Farm Bureau Federation.

The recent U.S. trade agreement with Southeast Asia is opening new opportunities in countries like Vietnam, the Philippines and Malaysia, she said, with Southeast Asia full of fast-growing economies with rising demand for high-quality feed ingredients.

"Diversifying our export base makes U.S. farmers less dependent on a single buyer and helps rebuild long-term global demand," said Parum.

In emailed comments to MarketWatch, White House Deputy Press Secretary Anna Kelly said: "President Trump cares deeply about our agriculture industry, and he has taken unprecedented action to level the playing field for American farmers after decades of unfair trading practices."

Kelly continued: "His success in opening the Chinese, European, British, Japanese, Vietnamese and numerous other foreign markets to U.S. soybeans and other agricultural exports will pay off in the long and short term by expanding opportunities for farmers to send made-in-America goods around the world."

The U.S. has been building up its domestic demand base for several years to replace Chinese business, but it still needs several more years to get there, said StoneX's Suderman. China's agreement to buy U.S. soybeans helps "bridge that gap" to keep demand strong while the domestic demand base is built, he added.

Parum believes the U.S.-China truce is a positive step toward "rebuilding trust and stability in trade."

"What matters now is enforcing the commitments so farmers have consistent, predictable access to markets," she said. "Steady markets, fair competition and enforceable trade agreements, both with China and growing partners in Southeast Asia, will keep the U.S. soybean sector strong for the long term."

-Myra P. Saefong

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: As California wildfire rages, Los Angeles threatened with floods, mudslides
data.description:
Key points:
  • Pack Fire consumes 3,400 acres, damages 15 structures, 1,400 evacuated
  • Heavy rain may aid in containing Pack Fire
  • Los Angeles faces flood, mudslide risks from weekend storms

By Rich McKay

A fast-moving wildfire raged across thousands of acres of California prairie near the Nevada border on Friday, while Los Angeles was in the throes of back-to-back storms that could trigger flooding and mudslides in areas scarred by January's devastating wildfires.

The new blaze, dubbed the Pack Fire, started on Thursday on scrubland in Mono County about 150 miles (241 km) southeast of Sacramento, according to the California Department of Forestry and Fire Protection, or Cal Fire.

Having consumed about 3,400 acres (1,375 hectares) as of Friday, the fire has damaged 15 structures and forced 1,400 people to evacuate, Cal Fire said.

Most have since been allowed to return to their homes, said Sergeant Brent Gillespie of the Mono County Sheriff's Office.

HEAVY RAIN EXPECTED TO HELP CONTAIN FIRE

The fire, which was 5% contained as of Friday, was burning about 15 miles from the Mammoth Lakes ski resort, where locals were preparing for the start of the season.

Forecasters say heavy rain pushing south from the San Francisco area could help firefighters control the blaze.

"The rain hasn't yet put a dent in it, but it stopped it from spreading," Gillespie said.

The storm, which brought moderate rain to the Los Angeles area on Friday, is expected to merge with a second weather system driving east off the Pacific on Saturday and Sunday, said Heather Zehr, a senior meteorologist with AccuWeather, a private forecasting company.

"It's the same storm that brought a messy commute to San Francisco Thursday, but it's undergoing a rapid change," Zehr said. "Los Angeles is getting some puddling rain on the streets today, but by Saturday, Southern California will see serious rainfall."

Downtown Los Angeles could get 2 to 4 inches (50 to 100 mm) of rain over the weekend, while other areas could see up to 8 or 10 inches, she said.

The steady deluge threatened to trigger mudslides in the burn-scarred land left after the January fires, one of the largest natural disasters to hit Los Angeles County.

"There will be flooding, flash floods and mudslides," Zehr said. "This is definitely a big deal."

January's Eaton and Palisades fires killed about 30 people and destroyed or damaged more than 16,000 homes and businesses. Up to 180,000 people were evacuated. AccuWeather estimates that damages and economic losses reached $250 billion.

The storms are expected to subside on Sunday, but more rain is expected later in the week.

data.title: TSX Reverses Early Session Losses
data.description:
The S&P/TSX Composite reversed early losses to hover above the flatline at the 30,250 mark on Friday afternoon, trimming some of the fallout from its steepest daily drop since April from the previous session as tech and financials recovered and energy names led gains.

Canadian Natural, Suncor, Imperial Oil and Cenovus added between 1.3% and 3% as oil prices jumped amid supply disruptions for Russian crude.

Major tech and financial firms pared most of their earlier sharp declines to trade flat or slightly lower, easing early-session pressure.

Hawkish comments from US Federal Reserve officials pared back hopes of a December rate cut, with several policymakers warning against further easing given persistent inflation and a still-resilient labour market.

On the data front wholesale sales rose 0.6% in September and manufacturing sales climbed about 3.3% in the same month.

data.title: International Paper to close two U.S. packaging plants amid weak demand
data.description:
International Paper on Friday said it will shut two U.S. packaging plants by January as the company cuts costs and consolidates operations amid weak demand.

The closures will affect facilities in Compton, California, and Louisville, Kentucky, impacting 218 employees. International Paper said production will shift to nearby sites.

"The company will work to minimize the impact on employees by using attrition, retirements and current vacancies at other International Paper locations," Tom Hamic, executive vice president and president of North American Packaging Solutions, said.

The company, which acquired UK-based DS Smith earlier this year, has been reshaping its portfolio. In August, it sold its global cellulose fibers unit to American Industrial Partners for $1.5 billion to focus on sustainable packaging.

International Paper, the world’s largest packaging company by revenue, posted a quarterly loss in October after taking a $1 billion impairment charge tied to the fibers unit sale.

President Donald Trump.

data.title: Spot basis bids flat, trade quiet
data.description:
Basis bids for hard red winter wheat were mostly unchanged in the U.S. Plains on Friday, dealers said.

  • Roughly one-third of the wheat in the U.S. Plains is too dry, though rains forecast to fall next week are expected to ease dryness.

  • Wheat is likely to go into dormancy around mid-December, an analyst note said.

  • Wheat futures ticked lower after a bearish supply and demand report from the U.S. Department of Agriculture.

  • K.C. December hard red winter wheat (KWZ25) was last down 12 cents to $5.13-3/4 per bushel.

data.title: CACI International Is Maintained at Buy by Truist Securities
Tectonic Financial, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Tectonic Financial, Inc., a diversified financial services company primarily operating in the United States with a significant focus on Texas, has released its Form 10-Q for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights robust financial performance and steady operational growth, driven by increased loan volumes and improved interest margins.

Financial Highlights

Net Income: Tectonic Financial reported a net income of $5.0 million for the three months ended September 30, 2025, marking a 19.2% increase compared to the same period last year. This growth was primarily due to a $2.7 million increase in net interest income and an $810,000 rise in non-interest income.

Diluted EPS: The diluted earnings per share (EPS) for the quarter stood at $0.73, up from $0.58 in the same period last year, reflecting the increase in net income.

Net Income (Nine Months): For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, the company reported a net income of $12.7 million, a 32.3% increase from the same period in 2024. This was driven by a $6.9 million increase in net interest income and a $3.8 million rise in non-interest income.

Diluted EPS (Nine Months): The diluted EPS for the nine-month period was $1.85, compared to $1.33 for the same period in 2024, indicating improved profitability.

Net Interest Income: The net interest income for the three months ended September 30, 2025, was $11.1 million, a 32.1% increase from the same period in 2024, attributed to higher loan volumes and lower average cost of interest-bearing liabilities.

Net Interest Margin: The net interest margin for the quarter was 4.39%, up from 4.25% in the same period in 2024, reflecting improved interest income relative to interest-earning assets.

Net Interest Income (Nine Months): For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, net interest income was $30.2 million, a 29.7% increase from the same period in 2024, driven by increased loan volumes and reduced interest costs.

Net Interest Margin (Nine Months): The net interest margin for the nine-month period was 4.37%, compared to 4.20% for the same period in 2024, indicating better management of interest-earning assets and liabilities.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: Tectonic Financial's primary reportable segments are Banking and Other Financial Services. The Banking segment includes commercial and consumer banking services, while the Other Financial Services segment encompasses activities such as investment advisory, brokerage, and trust services.

Geographical Performance: The company operates primarily in the United States, with a significant focus on the State of Texas, which is its primary market. Changes in the Texas economy, including unemployment rates, can significantly impact the company's operations.

Sales Units: The company reported an increase in the average volume of loans, particularly in SBA loans, which now comprise 65.1% of the total loan portfolio. This indicates a strong demand for SBA loans, which are a key focus area for the company.

Future Outlook: Tectonic Financial anticipates continued growth in its loan portfolio, particularly in SBA loans, despite potential economic challenges such as slower growth rates or a recession. The company is also focused on managing risks associated with its loan portfolio and maintaining adequate liquidity to meet depositor demands.

SEC Filing: Tectonic Financial, Inc. [ TECTP ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: RETRACTABLE TECHNOLOGIES INC SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
Retractable Technologies Inc., a company specializing in the manufacturing of safety medical devices, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant improvements in financial performance and outlines key operational developments. This summary provides an overview of the company's financial and business highlights as detailed in the filing.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales, net: $10.1 million, reflecting a slight decrease from the previous year due to a decline in domestic unit sales, although the average domestic selling price increased.
  • Gross profit (loss): $1.7 million, showing improvement from a loss in the previous year, primarily due to a favorable shift in product mix and lower tariff costs.
  • Loss from operations: $(3.7) million, an improvement from the previous year's loss, driven by higher gross margins and reduced tariff costs.
  • Net income (loss): $0.4 million, compared to a net loss in the previous year, aided by unrealized gains on debt and equity securities.
  • Basic earnings (loss) per share: $0.01, indicating a positive turnaround from a loss per share in the previous year.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Segments: Syringes accounted for 65.0% of sales in the first nine months of 2025, EasyPoint® products made up 31.3%, and other products, including IV safety catheters and blood collection products, comprised 3.7% of sales.
  • Geographical Performance: Domestic sales represented 91.1% of total revenues for the three months ended September 30, 2025, with a 4.6% decrease in domestic revenues compared to the same period in 2024. International revenues increased by 25% for the same period, despite a decline in average international selling price due to a shift in product mix.
  • Sales Units: Domestic unit sales decreased by 20.4% for the three months ended September 30, 2025, while international unit sales increased, leading to an overall decrease in unit sales by 12.4%. For the nine months ended September 30, 2025, domestic unit sales increased by 11.8%, and overall unit sales increased by 25.5%.
  • New Production Launches: The company has adapted equipment to increase domestic manufacturing capabilities, allowing for the production of 0.5 mL syringes domestically. Commercial quantities are expected to be available in the first half of 2026.
  • Future Outlook: The company anticipates that tariffs will continue to materially impact costs, with approximately $2.3 million spent on tariff expenses in the first nine months of 2025. Efforts to mitigate this include increasing domestic production and reducing reliance on imports, which may lead to higher manufacturing costs in the near term.

SEC Filing: RETRACTABLE TECHNOLOGIES INC [ RVP ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: KwikClick, Inc. SEC 10-Q Report
data.description:
KwikClick, Inc., a company specializing in custom design services and transaction platform integration, has released its Form 10-Q report for the third quarter of 2025. The report highlights significant financial and operational developments, reflecting the company's strategic focus on expanding its service offerings and enhancing revenue streams.

Financial Highlights

Net Revenue: $242,497. The increase in net revenue for the three months ended September 30, 2025, compared to the same period in 2024, is primarily due to the expansion of custom design services.

Cost of Sales: $106,043. The cost of sales increased due to higher labor costs associated with the expansion of custom design services.

Total Operating Costs and Expenses: $461,092. Operating expenses increased due to higher management and payroll costs, offset by a decrease in research and development expenses.

Net Loss: $(286,063). The net loss decreased compared to the same period in 2024, reflecting improved revenue performance.

Basic and Diluted Loss Per Share: $(0.07). The loss per share improved compared to the same period in 2024, reflecting a reduction in net loss.

Business Highlights

Revenue Segments: The company experienced a significant increase in net revenues due to the expansion of custom design services, which involve building custom software features for customers. This service is generally provided in addition to embedding the company's transaction platform into a customer's website.

Operating Expenses: There was an increase in management and payroll expenses, reflecting the company's efforts to support business growth. However, research and development expenses decreased, indicating a shift in resource allocation.

Future Outlook: The company plans to continue expanding its custom design services, which are expected to drive perpetual increases in brand services. This strategic focus aims to enhance the company's revenue streams and operational capabilities.

Geographical Performance: The company derives nearly all of its revenue from U.S.-based customers, with an immaterial amount coming from foreign-based customers. This highlights the company's strong domestic market presence.

SEC Filing: KwikClick, Inc. [ KWIK ] - 10-Q - Nov. 14, 2025

data.title: Gourmet supplier Innovative Food's Q3 revenue rises, driven by acquisitions
data.description:
Overview

  • Innovative Food Holdings Q3 revenue grows 3.5% yr/yr, driven by acquisitions

  • GAAP net income from continuing operations declines compared to Q3 2024

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific financial guidance for future periods in press release

Result Drivers

  • ACQUISITIONS DRIVE REVENUE - Revenue growth in Q3 was primarily driven by contributions from Golden Organics and LoCo Foods acquisitions

  • DIGITAL CHANNELS DECLINE - Revenue from Digital Channels declined by 4.5% in Q3, attributed to softness with the largest digital partner, though newer partnerships showed growth

  • LOCAL DISTRIBUTION GROWTH - Local Distribution increased by 33% in Q3, driven by acquisitions, but declined by 21.5% excluding acquisitions
